steps.title: Identify the core disagreement between the texts
steps.content: Text 1 criticizes Asimov's flat characters as a stylistic weakness. Text 2 rejects this critique, arguing that Asimov's minimal characterization is intentional—a deliberate choice to reflect his thematic aim that individuals don't matter across historical epochs.

steps.title: Find the choice that matches Text 2's position
steps.content: Choice C states the flatness should not be considered a flaw—capturing Text 2's argument that Asimov's minimal characterization is a deliberate choice serving thematic goals, not a weakness. The other choices either misattribute the cause (A), limit the scope (B), or misidentify the relationship (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
